title: Software Developers
exposure_rationale: While AI can generate boilerplate code and assist with debugging, complex software architecture, system design, and understanding business requirements remain deeply human tasks. Indian IT industry's service-oriented model means client interaction and project management add layers AI cannot fully replace.

title: Data Scientists
exposure_rationale: AI tools can automate routine data analysis and model building, but problem framing, feature engineering with domain expertise, and communicating insights to stakeholders require human judgment. The rapidly growing Indian data analytics market keeps demand high.

title: IT Support Specialists
exposure_rationale: AI chatbots and automated diagnostics can handle most tier-1 and tier-2 support tickets. Routine troubleshooting, password resets, and common issue resolution are highly automatable, though on-site hardware support and complex escalations still need humans.

title: Database Administrators
exposure_rationale: Cloud-managed database services and AI-powered optimization tools automate much of routine DBA work including backups, performance tuning, and monitoring. However, complex migration projects and security architecture still require experienced professionals.

title: Network Administrators
exposure_rationale: Software-defined networking and AI-based network monitoring reduce manual configuration needs. However, physical infrastructure management in India's rapidly expanding data center market and telecom networks requires hands-on expertise.

title: Cybersecurity Analysts
exposure_rationale: While AI enhances threat detection and automates routine scanning, the adversarial nature of cybersecurity means human creativity in threat hunting and incident response remains critical. India's growing digital infrastructure creates sustained demand.

title: Web Developers
exposure_rationale: AI code generation tools can now produce functional websites, landing pages, and standard web applications with minimal human input. Template-based web development work, which forms a large portion of India's freelance web dev market, is highly susceptible to automation.

title: Cloud Computing Specialists
exposure_rationale: Infrastructure-as-code and AI-driven cloud management automate provisioning and scaling, but complex multi-cloud architectures and cost optimization strategies require human expertise. India's cloud adoption boom sustains strong demand.

title: AI and Machine Learning Engineers
exposure_rationale: Paradoxically, AI engineers are among the least exposed as they build the AI systems. While AutoML tools simplify model development, designing novel architectures, understanding edge cases, and deploying production ML systems require deep expertise.

title: Mobile App Developers
exposure_rationale: AI-powered app builders and code generation tools can create standard mobile applications. However, complex native development, performance optimization, and integration with device-specific features still need skilled developers. India's app market remains highly competitive.

title: DevOps Engineers
exposure_rationale: While AI can automate CI/CD pipeline management and monitoring, the complexity of modern distributed systems and the need for judgment in deployment strategies keeps human DevOps engineers essential. Growing cloud adoption in India fuels demand.

title: UI/UX Designers
exposure_rationale: AI can generate UI mockups and design variations rapidly, but understanding user psychology, conducting research, and creating cohesive design systems require human creativity. India's growing startup ecosystem sustains demand for quality designers.

title: Quality Assurance Testers
exposure_rationale: AI-powered testing tools can automate test case generation, regression testing, and bug detection with increasing accuracy. Manual testing, which employs a large portion of India's QA workforce, is rapidly being automated. Only complex exploratory testing retains human advantage.

title: IT Project Managers
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with scheduling, resource allocation, and risk assessment, but stakeholder management, team leadership, and navigating India's complex client relationships in the IT services industry require strong interpersonal skills that AI cannot replicate.

title: Computer Hardware Engineers
exposure_rationale: Hardware design involves physical prototyping and testing that AI cannot perform. While simulation tools are AI-enhanced, the hands-on nature of hardware engineering in India's growing electronics manufacturing sector limits automation potential.

title: Data Engineers
exposure_rationale: AI can automate data pipeline generation and ETL processes, but designing scalable data architectures and handling India's diverse data sources and formats requires human expertise. Growing data infrastructure investments sustain demand.

title: Physicians and Surgeons
exposure_rationale: AI assists with diagnostics and treatment planning, but the physical examination, surgical procedures, and patient trust central to Indian healthcare cannot be automated. India's doctor shortage (1:1400 ratio) means demand far outstrips AI displacement.

title: Nurses (Staff Nurses)
exposure_rationale: Nursing requires hands-on patient care, emotional support, and physical tasks like administering medications and wound care. AI tools may help with documentation and monitoring, but bedside care in India's understaffed hospitals cannot be automated.

title: Pharmacists
exposure_rationale: AI can check drug interactions, suggest alternatives, and automate inventory management. In India, many pharmacy tasks are routine dispensing that could be automated, though regulatory requirements and patient counseling provide some protection.

title: Dentists
exposure_rationale: Dental procedures require manual dexterity and physical presence. While AI aids in diagnostics through X-ray analysis and treatment planning, the hands-on nature of dentistry in India's largely private practice model limits automation.

title: Physiotherapists
exposure_rationale: Physical rehabilitation requires hands-on manipulation, real-time assessment of patient movement, and motivational coaching. AI may assist with exercise planning but cannot replace the tactile, adaptive nature of physiotherapy sessions.

title: Lab Technicians (Medical)
exposure_rationale: AI-powered diagnostic equipment can automate blood analysis, pathology slide reading, and routine test interpretation. Many standardized lab procedures in India's diagnostic chains are increasingly automated, though sample handling remains manual.

title: Community Health Workers (ASHA)
exposure_rationale: ASHA workers perform door-to-door visits in rural India, provide maternal health support, and serve as the last-mile healthcare link. Their work is inherently physical, community-based, and requires local knowledge that AI cannot provide.

title: Paramedics and EMTs
exposure_rationale: Emergency medical response requires physical intervention, rapid on-scene decision making, and patient transport. India's growing ambulance network (108 services) relies entirely on human responders for life-saving interventions.

title: Hospital Administrators
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize scheduling, resource allocation, and billing processes. However, managing India's complex hospital ecosystems involving government regulations, insurance negotiations, and staff management requires human leadership and judgment.

title: Medical Transcriptionists
exposure_rationale: AI speech-to-text and natural language processing can transcribe medical dictation with high accuracy. India's large medical transcription outsourcing industry is rapidly declining as automated solutions replace human transcriptionists.

title: Ayurvedic Practitioners
exposure_rationale: Ayurvedic practice involves personalized pulse diagnosis (Nadi Pariksha), herbal formulation, and holistic patient assessment rooted in traditional knowledge. The cultural and tactile nature of this practice in India makes it resistant to AI automation.

title: Veterinarians
exposure_rationale: Veterinary work involves physical examination and treatment of animals, surgery, and fieldwork in India's large livestock sector. AI can assist diagnostics but cannot replace hands-on animal care in rural settings.

title: School Teachers (Primary)
exposure_rationale: While AI tutoring tools can supplement learning, primary education in India requires nurturing young children, managing classrooms, and providing social-emotional development that AI cannot replicate. India's teacher shortage in government schools sustains demand.

title: School Teachers (Secondary)
exposure_rationale: AI can automate grading, generate lesson plans, and provide personalized tutoring, partially displacing routine teaching tasks. However, classroom management, student mentoring, and adapting to India's diverse learning environments require human teachers.

title: College Professors
exposure_rationale: AI can generate lecture content and automate assessment, but original research, mentoring graduate students, and navigating India's complex academic environment require human expertise. UGC regulations mandate human faculty for accreditation.

title: Private Tutors
exposure_rationale: AI tutoring platforms like Byju's, Vedantu, and emerging LLM-based tutors can provide personalized instruction at scale for a fraction of the cost. India's massive private tutoring market is highly vulnerable as AI tutors improve in quality and accessibility.

title: Special Education Teachers
exposure_rationale: Working with children who have learning disabilities, autism, or physical challenges requires deep empathy, patience, and adaptive teaching strategies. The highly personalized, physically interactive nature of special education in India limits AI displacement.

title: Anganwadi Workers
exposure_rationale: Anganwadi workers provide nutrition, preschool education, and health services in India's rural areas through direct community engagement. Their work involves cooking meals, monitoring child growth, and home visits that cannot be automated.

title: Vocational Trainers
exposure_rationale: Vocational training in trades like welding, plumbing, and electrical work requires hands-on demonstration and supervision. While theory can be AI-delivered, India's Skill India initiative emphasizes practical training that needs human instructors.

title: Education Technology Specialists
exposure_rationale: AI is both a tool and competitor for EdTech specialists. They design AI-enhanced learning systems, but AI can also generate educational content and platform features. India's booming EdTech sector creates demand while AI automates some roles.

title: Farmers (Crop Cultivation)
exposure_rationale: Indian farming remains predominantly manual, with small landholdings (avg 1.08 hectares) limiting mechanization. AI-powered crop advisory apps exist but reach is limited. Physical labor in planting, irrigation, and harvesting in India's diverse terrain resists automation.

title: Agricultural Labourers
exposure_rationale: Manual farm labor including transplanting, weeding, and harvesting is physically intensive and performed across India's varied topography. While mechanization is slowly increasing, the small farm sizes and rural employment needs limit AI/automation impact.

title: Dairy Farmers
exposure_rationale: India's dairy sector (world's largest milk producer) relies on small-holder farmers managing 2-5 animals. Daily milking, feeding, and animal health monitoring are hands-on tasks. AI apps for cattle management exist but adoption is minimal in rural India.

title: Fishermen and Aquaculture Workers
exposure_rationale: Fishing in India involves manual net casting, boat operation, and catch handling in coastal and inland waters. Aquaculture requires physical pond management. AI weather/fish-finding tools help but cannot replace the physical labor involved.

title: Agricultural Scientists
exposure_rationale: AI accelerates crop research through genomic analysis and yield prediction models. However, field trials, soil analysis, and developing crop varieties suited to India's diverse agro-climatic zones require hands-on scientific expertise.

title: Plantation Workers
exposure_rationale: Tea picking, coffee harvesting, and rubber tapping in India's plantations require skilled manual labor on steep terrain. These tasks involve dexterity and judgment that current automation technology cannot replicate in India's plantation geography.

title: Horticulturists
exposure_rationale: While AI can optimize irrigation and pest management schedules, hands-on cultivation, grafting, and landscape design require physical skill. India's growing floriculture and fruit export sectors need human expertise for quality control.

title: Poultry Farmers
exposure_rationale: India's poultry industry is increasingly organized but still involves significant manual work in feeding, cleaning, vaccination, and monitoring bird health. Automation exists in large farms but most Indian poultry operations are small-scale.

title: Factory Workers (Assembly Line)
exposure_rationale: Repetitive assembly tasks are prime targets for robotic automation. India's Make in India push is bringing more automated factories, though low labor costs have slowed the transition. Electronics and auto assembly are most exposed sectors.

title: Textile Workers
exposure_rationale: India's massive textile sector spans high automation (spinning mills) to manual work (handloom weaving). Power loom operations are moderately automatable, but garment stitching and quality inspection require human dexterity. Export competition drives automation adoption.

title: Welders and Fabricators
exposure_rationale: Robotic welding is common in automotive but India's construction and small-scale manufacturing sectors rely heavily on manual welders for custom jobs. Structural welding in varied environments and repair work require human adaptability.

title: CNC Machine Operators
exposure_rationale: AI-powered CNC machines can self-optimize cutting parameters and detect tool wear. Programming is being simplified by AI, reducing the skill barrier. However, setup, monitoring, and handling complex custom parts still need operators in India's growing precision manufacturing sector.

title: Quality Control Inspectors
exposure_rationale: Computer vision and AI-based inspection systems can detect defects faster and more consistently than humans. India's manufacturing sector is increasingly adopting automated quality systems, especially in pharma, auto, and electronics industries.

title: Industrial Engineers
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize production schedules, supply chains, and layout planning. However, implementing process improvements, managing shop floors, and navigating India's diverse manufacturing environments require human judgment and physical presence.

title: Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Workers
exposure_rationale: India's pharma sector (pharmacy of the world) is automating production lines, but strict regulatory compliance, cleanroom protocols, and batch monitoring require human oversight. Generic drug manufacturing involves complex quality assurance.

title: Automobile Assembly Workers
exposure_rationale: Auto assembly is one of the most robotics-ready sectors globally. India's major auto plants (Maruti, Tata, Hyundai) are rapidly automating welding, painting, and assembly. The shift to EVs may further accelerate automation of simpler manufacturing processes.

title: Food Processing Workers
exposure_rationale: India's food processing sector spans from highly automated (biscuits, beverages) to manual (spice grinding, pickle making). Sorting, grading, and packaging are automatable, but India's diverse cuisine and small-scale processors limit full automation.

title: Plant Managers
exposure_rationale: AI dashboards provide real-time production insights and predictive maintenance alerts. However, managing workforce issues, safety protocols, and operational decisions in India's complex labor environment requires experienced human leadership.

title: Construction Labourers
exposure_rationale: India's construction sector is one of the largest employers, relying heavily on manual labor for tasks like mixing, carrying materials, and site preparation. Low labor costs and the physical nature of work in varied Indian terrain limit automation adoption.

title: Masons and Bricklayers
exposure_rationale: Bricklaying and masonry in India involve custom work on diverse building designs, often in confined spaces with irregular foundations. While robotic bricklaying exists globally, India's construction practices and low labor costs make automation unlikely in the near term.

title: Carpenters
exposure_rationale: Custom furniture making and on-site woodworking require manual skill and creative judgment. CNC routers handle factory production, but India's demand for bespoke carpentry in homes and offices keeps manual carpenters essential.

title: Plumbers
exposure_rationale: Plumbing involves working in cramped, varied spaces with unpredictable pipe configurations. Each installation and repair is unique. India's growing urban infrastructure and building boom ensures sustained demand for skilled plumbers.

title: Electricians
exposure_rationale: Electrical work requires navigating building-specific wiring, safety compliance, and troubleshooting in varied environments. India's electrification push, solar installations, and EV charging infrastructure create growing demand that AI cannot serve.

title: Civil Engineers
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with structural analysis, project scheduling, and BIM modeling. However, site supervision, managing India's regulatory approvals, and adapting designs to local conditions require human judgment. Infrastructure spending under PM Gati Shakti sustains demand.

title: Architects
exposure_rationale: AI can generate architectural designs, floor plans, and 3D renderings rapidly. Parametric design tools automate much of the computational work. However, understanding client needs, cultural context, and India's vastu/regulatory requirements needs human architects.

title: Interior Designers
exposure_rationale: AI tools can generate interior design concepts, material suggestions, and 3D visualizations. However, understanding client taste, managing renovations on-site, and sourcing materials in India's fragmented market require human designers.

title: Crane Operators
exposure_rationale: Operating heavy machinery requires real-time spatial judgment, safety awareness, and adaptation to weather and site conditions. Autonomous cranes exist but India's complex construction sites with varied terrain make full automation difficult.

title: Painting and Finishing Workers
exposure_rationale: Interior and exterior painting involves surface preparation, color mixing, and application on irregular surfaces in existing buildings. The physical dexterity and adaptability needed in India's varied building stock makes this work resistant to automation.

title: Chartered Accountants
exposure_rationale: AI can automate tax computation, financial analysis, audit procedures, and compliance reporting. India's GST returns and ITR filing are increasingly automated. While advisory and complex forensic auditing need humans, routine CA work is highly exposed.

title: Bank Clerks
exposure_rationale: UPI, digital banking, and AI chatbots have dramatically reduced the need for counter transactions. India's digital payments revolution (UPI processing 12B+ transactions/month) is making branch-level clerical work increasingly obsolete.

title: Financial Analysts
exposure_rationale: AI can process financial data, generate reports, build models, and identify market trends faster than humans. Routine financial analysis and reporting in India's growing financial sector is highly automatable, though strategic advisory retains human value.

title: Insurance Agents
exposure_rationale: Online insurance platforms (PolicyBazaar, etc.) and AI-driven policy recommendations are replacing traditional insurance agents in India. Automated underwriting and claims processing further reduce the need for human intermediaries.

title: Stock Brokers
exposure_rationale: Algorithmic trading, robo-advisors, and discount brokerages (Zerodha, Groww) have transformed India's stock market. AI handles trade execution, portfolio management, and market analysis, leaving very limited scope for traditional brokers.

title: Tax Consultants
exposure_rationale: AI tools can prepare tax returns, identify deductions, and ensure compliance with India's complex GST and income tax regulations. However, tax planning for complex business structures and representing clients before authorities still needs human expertise.

title: Management Consultants
exposure_rationale: AI can generate market analysis, process data, and create presentations. However, client relationship management, organizational change management, and strategic recommendations tailored to India's business environment require human consultants.

title: Auditors
exposure_rationale: AI can analyze financial records, detect anomalies, and automate compliance checks at scale. India's statutory audit requirements provide some protection, but routine audit procedures are increasingly automated, particularly in the Big Four firms.

title: Investment Bankers
exposure_rationale: AI automates financial modeling, valuation analysis, and deal screening. However, relationship building, deal negotiations, and understanding India's complex regulatory landscape for M&A transactions require experienced human bankers.

title: Actuaries
exposure_rationale: AI and machine learning excel at the statistical modeling and risk analysis central to actuarial work. While strategic risk assessment and regulatory compliance in India's insurance sector need human judgment, core computational tasks are highly automatable.

title: Real Estate Agents
exposure_rationale: Online property portals (99acres, MagicBricks) and virtual tours reduce the need for agents in property discovery. However, India's complex property documentation, negotiation culture, and verification processes still need human intermediaries.

title: Business Analysts
exposure_rationale: AI can automate data analysis, generate reports, and identify process improvements. However, understanding business context, stakeholder management, and translating requirements in India's IT services sector still requires human analysts.

title: IAS Officers (Civil Services)
exposure_rationale: District administration, policy formulation, and crisis management require human judgment, empathy, and leadership. While AI can assist with data analysis for decision-making, the governance and public-facing nature of IAS work in India cannot be automated.

title: Police Officers
exposure_rationale: Policing requires physical presence, community engagement, and on-ground law enforcement. AI assists with surveillance and crime analysis, but India's community policing model and varied law-and-order situations need human officers.

title: Military Personnel
exposure_rationale: While AI enhances military intelligence and drone operations, ground combat, border patrolling in India's challenging terrain (Himalayas, deserts), and peacekeeping operations require human soldiers. India's large military maintains human-centric operations.

title: Government Clerks
exposure_rationale: Digital India initiatives are automating document processing, record keeping, and citizen services. E-governance portals and AI-powered systems handle tasks previously done by clerks. India's massive bureaucracy is slowly but steadily digitizing routine clerical work.

title: Postal Workers
exposure_rationale: Email and digital communication have drastically reduced mail volume. India Post is diversifying into banking and e-commerce logistics, but traditional postal sorting and delivery roles are declining. Automated sorting machines further reduce manual work.

title: Railway Workers
exposure_rationale: Indian Railways employs millions across operations, maintenance, and signaling. While automatic signaling and computerized reservation reduce some roles, track maintenance, station operations, and train operations still require human workers.

title: Judges and Magistrates
exposure_rationale: While AI can assist with case research, precedent analysis, and docket management, judicial decision-making requires legal interpretation, ethical reasoning, and understanding of Indian constitutional values that cannot be delegated to AI.

title: Firefighters
exposure_rationale: Firefighting involves physical rescue operations, hazardous material handling, and emergency response in unpredictable environments. AI-enhanced fire detection helps prevention, but active firefighting in India's dense urban areas requires human responders.

title: Truck Drivers
exposure_rationale: While autonomous trucking is advancing globally, India's chaotic road conditions, diverse terrain, and infrastructure limitations make full self-driving trucks unlikely soon. However, platooning and highway autonomy may gradually reduce driver needs over the long term.

title: Auto/Taxi Drivers
exposure_rationale: India's complex urban traffic, narrow streets, and unstructured driving environment make autonomous taxis extremely challenging. Ride-hailing apps (Ola, Uber) changed the business model but still need human drivers. Full automation is decades away for Indian roads.

title: Delivery Executives
exposure_rationale: India's e-commerce boom (Flipkart, Amazon, Zomato, Swiggy) has created massive demand for delivery workers. While drone delivery is being piloted, India's dense urban areas, apartment buildings, and last-mile complexity make human delivery workers essential.

title: Airline Pilots
exposure_rationale: Modern aircraft have extensive autopilot systems, but takeoff, landing, and emergency handling require human pilots. Regulatory requirements mandate human cockpit crews. India's growing aviation market (fastest growing globally) sustains pilot demand.

title: Warehouse Workers
exposure_rationale: Amazon and Flipkart warehouses in India are adopting robotic picking and automated sorting systems. Routine tasks like inventory management and order fulfillment are increasingly automated, though India's lower labor costs slow the transition versus Western markets.

title: Logistics Coordinators
exposure_rationale: AI-powered logistics platforms optimize routing, load planning, and shipment tracking. Many coordination tasks are being automated in India's rapidly digitizing supply chain sector. However, managing India's complex last-mile logistics still needs human oversight.

title: Bus Drivers
exposure_rationale: Urban and intercity bus driving in India involves navigating crowded streets, mixed traffic, and passenger management. Autonomous buses are being tested in controlled environments but India's road conditions make widespread deployment unlikely in the near future.

title: Railway Engine Drivers
exposure_rationale: Automated train operation systems are being deployed on metro lines across Indian cities. While mainline trains still need drivers for safety, the introduction of KAVACH automatic train protection system and metro automation is gradually reducing the role.

title: Supply Chain Managers
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes demand forecasting, inventory management, and supplier selection. However, managing relationships with India's diverse supplier base, handling disruptions, and strategic decision-making require experienced human managers.

title: Shop Keepers and Retail Workers
exposure_rationale: India's 12 million kirana stores form the backbone of retail. While e-commerce grows, the personal relationship-based shopping model, credit systems, and neighborhood convenience of small shops provide resilience. Self-checkout and cashierless stores are minimal in India.

title: Hotel Managers
exposure_rationale: AI can handle reservations, revenue management, and guest service chatbots. However, guest experience management, staff leadership, and handling complex situations in India's growing hospitality sector require human hotel managers.

title: Chefs and Cooks
exposure_rationale: Cooking requires tactile skills, taste judgment, and creativity. India's diverse regional cuisines with complex spice combinations and traditional techniques make cooking highly resistant to automation. Restaurant kitchens remain fundamentally human workspaces.

title: Waiters and Restaurant Staff
exposure_rationale: While QR code menus and ordering apps reduce some tasks, Indian dining culture values personal service and hospitality. Table service, food delivery within restaurants, and customer interaction remain human-centric in India's restaurant industry.

title: Tour Guides
exposure_rationale: AI audio guides and virtual tours can provide historical information, but the personal touch, local knowledge, and adaptive storytelling of human guides remain valued. India's tourism sector benefits from guides who navigate complex cultural contexts.

title: Barbers and Beauty Professionals
exposure_rationale: Haircutting, styling, and beauty treatments are inherently physical, creative, and personalized services. India's large salon industry from neighborhood barber shops to premium salons relies entirely on human skill and cannot be automated.

title: Event Managers
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with planning, vendor matching, and logistics coordination. However, managing India's elaborate wedding and event industry requires on-ground coordination, creative direction, and handling real-time challenges that need human judgment.

title: Street Vendors
exposure_rationale: Street vending involves physical preparation and selling of food, goods, and services in public spaces. This informal sector work in India requires physical presence, customer interaction, and adaptability to weather and location that cannot be automated.

title: E-commerce Sellers
exposure_rationale: AI tools automate product listing, pricing optimization, and customer service on platforms like Amazon and Flipkart. However, product sourcing, quality management, and brand building for India's diverse consumer base still need human sellers.

title: Customer Service Representatives
exposure_rationale: AI chatbots and voice assistants handle an increasing share of customer queries across India's telecom, banking, and e-commerce sectors. India's BPO industry, once a major employer, is seeing significant job displacement from AI-powered customer support systems.

title: Housekeeping Staff
exposure_rationale: Hotel and office housekeeping involves physical cleaning, room setup, and maintaining standards in varied spaces. India's growing hospitality and corporate office sectors rely on human housekeeping staff for tasks robots cannot efficiently perform.

title: Journalists
exposure_rationale: AI can generate news articles, summarize events, and create data-driven reports. India's news industry is already using AI for regional language content generation. However, investigative journalism, source cultivation, and editorial judgment retain human value.

title: Television Anchors
exposure_rationale: AI-generated news anchors are being tested by Indian news channels. While live debate moderation and breaking news coverage need human anchors, routine bulletin reading is increasingly automatable with AI avatars and text-to-speech technology.

title: Actors and Performers
exposure_rationale: AI can generate digital likenesses and dub voices, affecting background artists and dubbing professionals. However, lead performances in Bollywood and regional cinema require human emotion and creativity. India's thriving film industry values star power.

title: Graphic Designers
exposure_rationale: AI image generators (Midjourney, DALL-E) can create logos, social media graphics, and marketing materials rapidly. India's large graphic design workforce, especially those doing template-based work for small businesses, faces significant displacement.

title: Content Writers and Copywriters
exposure_rationale: AI can generate blog posts, product descriptions, social media content, and marketing copy in multiple Indian languages. India's content writing industry, which grew with digital marketing, is highly vulnerable as AI produces comparable quality at lower cost.

title: Video Editors
exposure_rationale: AI tools can automate basic video editing tasks like cutting, color correction, and subtitling. India's growing YouTube and social media content ecosystem still needs creative editors, but routine editing work is increasingly handled by AI.

title: Social Media Managers
exposure_rationale: AI can schedule posts, generate content ideas, analyze engagement metrics, and create social media copy. However, brand voice development, crisis management, and community building for India's diverse digital audience need human social media managers.

title: Photographers
exposure_rationale: AI can enhance and edit photos, but capturing moments at weddings, events, and commercial shoots requires physical presence and creative vision. India's massive wedding photography market provides resilience, though stock photography is being displaced by AI.

title: Animators
exposure_rationale: AI can generate character animations, in-between frames, and motion graphics. India's growing animation industry (Chhota Bheem, etc.) uses AI for efficiency, but creative direction, character design, and storytelling still need human animators.

title: Music Directors and Composers
exposure_rationale: AI can generate background scores and simple melodies, but India's film music industry values distinctive human creativity, cultural sensitivity, and the unique artistic vision that composers bring to Bollywood and regional cinema.

title: Advocates (Lawyers)
exposure_rationale: AI can draft contracts, review documents, and conduct legal research across India's vast case law. However, courtroom advocacy, client counseling, and navigating India's complex legal system with its multiple court hierarchies require human lawyers.

title: Legal Advisors (Corporate)
exposure_rationale: AI can automate contract review, compliance monitoring, and due diligence. India's growing corporate sector needs legal advisors, but routine legal documentation and regulatory compliance work is increasingly handled by AI-powered legal tech platforms.

title: Paralegals
exposure_rationale: AI legal research tools can search case law, prepare document summaries, and organize case files faster than humans. India's paralegal work involving document preparation and legal research is highly susceptible to AI automation.

title: Court Clerks
exposure_rationale: India's e-Courts project is digitizing case filing, record management, and scheduling. AI-assisted case management systems are reducing the need for manual clerical work in India's court system, though the transition is gradual.

title: Patent Attorneys
exposure_rationale: AI can search prior art, draft patent claims, and analyze patent landscapes efficiently. However, strategic patent portfolio management and prosecution before India's patent office require human expertise in both law and technology.

title: Research Scientists
exposure_rationale: AI accelerates literature review, data analysis, and hypothesis generation. However, designing experiments, interpreting results in context, and original scientific thinking cannot be automated. India's growing R&D investment (CSIR, DRDO, IITs) sustains demand.

title: Biotechnologists
exposure_rationale: AI aids in drug discovery, genome analysis, and protein folding prediction, but lab work, clinical trials, and regulatory navigation require human scientists. India's pharma and biotech sectors are growing rapidly with AI as a complementary tool.

title: Space Scientists (ISRO)
exposure_rationale: Space mission design, satellite engineering, and launch operations require specialized human expertise. While AI assists with trajectory calculations and data analysis, India's ambitious space program (Chandrayaan, Gaganyaan) relies on human scientists.

title: Statisticians
exposure_rationale: AI and machine learning can perform statistical analysis, build predictive models, and identify patterns faster than human statisticians. India's growing data economy automates much routine statistical work, though experimental design and interpretation need humans.

title: Environmental Scientists
exposure_rationale: Environmental assessment requires field sampling, site visits, and understanding local ecosystems. While AI aids in satellite data analysis and pollution modeling, India's environmental challenges (river cleanup, air quality) need on-ground scientists.

title: Chemists
exposure_rationale: AI accelerates molecular simulation and compound screening but laboratory synthesis, analytical testing, and quality control require hands-on expertise. India's pharma and chemical industries need chemists for regulatory compliance and production.

title: Tailors and Garment Workers
exposure_rationale: Custom tailoring requires measuring, cutting, and stitching garments to individual specifications. India's strong culture of tailored clothing (especially for women's wear and formal wear) ensures sustained demand for skilled tailors.

title: Jewellers and Goldsmiths
exposure_rationale: Handcrafted jewellery making involves intricate metalwork, stone setting, and design that requires years of training. India's massive gold jewellery market values handmade craftsmanship, though CAD design is supplementing traditional methods.

title: Handloom Weavers
exposure_rationale: Handloom weaving is a traditional craft producing unique textiles (Banarasi, Kanjeevaram, Ikat). The artistic nature and cultural value of handwoven fabrics cannot be replicated by AI. However, the occupation faces decline due to competition from power looms.

title: Auto Mechanics
exposure_rationale: Vehicle repair requires physical diagnosis, disassembly, and repair in varied conditions. While AI diagnostics help identify issues, the hands-on repair work and India's large fleet of older vehicles keep mechanics essential. EV transition may change skill requirements.

title: Air Conditioning Technicians
exposure_rationale: AC installation, repair, and maintenance involve physical work in varied building environments. India's rapidly growing AC market (single-digit penetration rate) ensures strong demand. Smart diagnostics help but cannot replace hands-on technical work.

title: Mobile Phone Repair Technicians
exposure_rationale: Software issues can be diagnosed remotely, and phones are increasingly designed to be disposable rather than repairable. India's Right to Repair movement and cost-conscious consumers sustain some demand, but the shift to unrepairable designs threatens this occupation.

title: Solar Panel Installers
exposure_rationale: Solar panel installation requires roof assessment, mounting, wiring, and commissioning at varied sites. India's massive solar target (500 GW by 2030) creates enormous demand for skilled installers that automation cannot replace.

title: Blacksmiths
exposure_rationale: Traditional blacksmithing involves heating, hammering, and shaping metal by hand. While industrial forging is automated, rural India's agricultural tool and hardware needs sustain demand for village blacksmiths, though the occupation is declining.

title: Leather Workers
exposure_rationale: Leather cutting, stitching, and finishing for shoes and goods requires skilled manual work. India's large leather export industry (Kanpur, Chennai) relies on craftspeople, though machine cutting is automating some processes.

title: Domestic Workers (Housemaids)
exposure_rationale: Household cleaning, cooking, and childcare involve physical tasks in private homes with varied layouts and family preferences. India's middle-class households rely heavily on domestic help, and this deeply personal, physical work cannot be automated.

title: Security Guards
exposure_rationale: While CCTV and AI surveillance systems enhance monitoring, physical security presence at India's residential complexes, offices, and public spaces provides deterrence and emergency response that cameras alone cannot. However, routine gatekeeping may be reduced by smart access systems.

title: Drivers (Personal)
exposure_rationale: Personal drivers navigate India's complex traffic, manage vehicle maintenance, and provide flexible transportation. Self-driving cars are decades away from India's roads. The personal driver culture among upper-middle class families remains strong.

title: Sanitation Workers
exposure_rationale: Manual cleaning of drains, sewers, and public spaces in India involves hazardous physical work that must be done by humans. While mechanization of sewer cleaning is mandated, implementation is slow and most sanitation work remains manual.

title: Cooks (Household)
exposure_rationale: Household cooking in India involves preparing diverse regional meals, adapting to family preferences, and working with fresh ingredients in varied kitchen setups. This inherently manual, creative, and personalized work has zero AI automation potential.

title: Childcare Workers (Ayahs)
exposure_rationale: Caring for infants and children requires physical attention, emotional bonding, feeding, and safety monitoring. India's working families depend on ayahs and nannies for hands-on childcare that cannot be replaced by any technology.

title: Yoga Instructors
exposure_rationale: Yoga instruction requires physical demonstration, posture correction, and spiritual/mental guidance. While AI apps provide basic guidance, the personal connection and hands-on correction central to Indian yoga tradition cannot be automated.

title: Washermen (Dhobis)
exposure_rationale: While washing machines reduce demand, India's dhobi system provides laundry services including ironing and starch finishing that machines alone cannot. Urban laundry services are growing but traditional dhobis face competition from modern laundry chains.

title: Sugarcane Farmers
exposure_rationale: Sugarcane farming in India relies on manual harvesting and traditional methods. Physical field work and monsoon-dependent cultivation make AI replacement virtually impossible.

title: Cotton Farmers
exposure_rationale: Cotton cultivation in India involves manual picking and traditional farming techniques. The physical nature of fieldwork and pest management through manual inspection limits AI applicability.

title: Horticulture Workers
exposure_rationale: Fruit and vegetable cultivation requires hands-on care, pruning, and harvest timing knowledge. While precision agriculture tools exist, Indian horticulture remains largely manual.

title: Sericulture Workers
exposure_rationale: Silk production involves rearing silkworms and processing cocoons. The biological nature of the work and artisanal skills make AI automation nearly impossible.

title: Tea Garden Workers
exposure_rationale: Tea plucking requires identifying the right leaves by hand, and Indias steep terrain plantations make mechanization impractical. AI has minimal relevance to this physical task.

title: Spice Farmers
exposure_rationale: Growing spices like turmeric, cardamom, and pepper requires knowledge of microclimates and hand-harvesting. AI-based crop monitoring is emerging but adoption is minimal.

title: Tile and Marble Workers
exposure_rationale: Tile laying and marble fitting are precision manual trades requiring on-site measurement and aesthetic judgement. The custom nature of each installation limits automation potential.

title: Scaffolding Workers
exposure_rationale: Scaffolding erection is entirely physical work requiring balance and on-site judgement about structural loads. No viable AI replacement exists for this work.

title: Road Construction Workers
exposure_rationale: Road building involves heavy manual labor - laying tar, compacting earth, and breaking rocks. While heavy machinery assists, the manual labor component in India remains very high.

title: Nannies and Childcare Workers
exposure_rationale: Childcare requires emotional intelligence, physical presence, and moment-to-moment judgement about safety. AI cannot provide the human connection children need.

title: Laundry Workers (Dhobi)
exposure_rationale: Traditional laundry work in India involves hand-washing, ironing, and garment care. The physical nature and low cost of labor makes automation economically unviable.

title: Event and Wedding Planners
exposure_rationale: Wedding planning requires creative vision and vendor coordination but AI can assist with scheduling, budgeting, and design mockups. The personal touch in Indian weddings limits full automation.

title: Fitness Trainers
exposure_rationale: AI fitness apps are growing but personal trainers provide motivation, form correction, and customized programs. The growing Indian fitness market values human coaches.

title: Priests and Religious Workers
exposure_rationale: Religious ceremonies require deep scriptural knowledge, spiritual authority, and community trust. AI cannot perform pujas, conduct weddings, or provide spiritual counsel.

title: University Professors
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with grading, content creation, and research. However, mentoring, original research direction, and classroom discussion facilitation remain distinctly human in Indian universities.

title: Coaching Institute Teachers
exposure_rationale: Test prep coaching for JEE/NEET/UPSC is increasingly moving online. AI tutoring systems can personalize practice and provide instant feedback, threatening this large Indian industry.

title: Microfinance Field Officers
exposure_rationale: Field officers build trust in rural communities and assess creditworthiness through personal visits. Digital lending is growing but rural India still relies on human intermediaries.

title: LIC and Insurance Agents
exposure_rationale: Online insurance platforms and AI-powered policy recommendations are reducing the role of traditional insurance agents. However, trust-based selling remains important in rural India.

title: Municipal Corporation Workers
exposure_rationale: Municipal workers handle waste collection, drain cleaning, and urban maintenance. The physical, on-ground nature of this work in Indian cities has minimal AI exposure.

title: ASHA Health Workers
exposure_rationale: ASHA workers provide grassroots healthcare in rural India - conducting surveys, distributing medicines, and connecting communities to health systems. The human trust element is irreplaceable.

title: Traditional Medicine Practitioners
exposure_rationale: Ayurveda and Unani practitioners rely on pulse diagnosis, personalized herbal formulations, and holistic assessment. This traditional knowledge system is difficult to codify for AI.

title: Medical Lab Technicians
exposure_rationale: AI-powered diagnostic tools can analyze blood tests, pathology slides, and imaging faster than humans. Indian diagnostic chains are rapidly adopting automated analysis.

title: Technical Support Engineers
exposure_rationale: AI chatbots and automated troubleshooting are rapidly replacing L1/L2 support roles. Indias massive BPO tech support industry faces significant disruption from conversational AI.

title: Manual Testers
exposure_rationale: AI-powered test automation tools can generate test cases, perform visual regression testing, and find bugs. Manual testing roles in Indian IT companies are declining rapidly.

title: Notaries and Document Writers
exposure_rationale: Document drafting and notarization are increasingly digitized. AI can generate standard legal documents, and e-signatures reduce the need for physical notaries.

title: Court Clerks and Stenographers
exposure_rationale: AI transcription services can replace court stenography. Digital case management systems reduce clerical work. Indian courts are slowly digitizing, threatening these roles.

title: Legal Research Associates
exposure_rationale: AI legal research tools can analyze case law, identify precedents, and summarize judgments faster than human researchers. Indian law firms are adopting these tools.

title: Steel Mill Workers
exposure_rationale: Steel production involves operating heavy machinery in extreme conditions. While process automation exists, Indian steel mills still rely heavily on manual labor for material handling.

title: Bidi and Cigarette Workers
exposure_rationale: Bidi rolling is entirely manual work done mostly by women in home settings. The cottage industry nature and extremely low wages make automation economically unviable.

title: Electronics Assembly Workers
exposure_rationale: Indias growing electronics manufacturing under PLI scheme uses semi-automated lines. Full automation is expensive but AI-guided quality inspection is being adopted.

title: Dubbing Artists
exposure_rationale: AI voice cloning and dubbing tools can replicate voices in multiple languages. Indias massive film dubbing industry across 20+ languages faces major disruption.

title: News Anchors and Reporters
exposure_rationale: AI-generated news summaries and virtual anchors are emerging. However, investigative journalism, live reporting, and editorial judgement remain human strengths.

title: Print Journalists
exposure_rationale: AI can write news articles, summarize events, and even generate opinion pieces. Combined with declining print readership in India, this profession faces significant pressure.

title: Delivery Workers (Gig Economy)
exposure_rationale: Food and package delivery requires navigating Indian streets, handling cash, and customer interaction. While route optimization uses AI, the physical delivery remains entirely human.

title: Travel Agents
exposure_rationale: Online travel platforms like MakeMyTrip and AI-powered booking assistants have devastated traditional travel agencies. Complex itinerary planning is one remaining human edge.

title: Florists and Garland Makers
exposure_rationale: Making garlands and flower arrangements for temples, weddings, and festivals is entirely manual artisanal work. The perishable nature and cultural significance keep this deeply human.

title: Space and Aerospace Engineers
exposure_rationale: ISRO and private space companies use AI for simulations and trajectory planning, but spacecraft design, testing, and mission control require deep human engineering expertise.

title: Potters and Ceramic Workers
exposure_rationale: Traditional pottery is a manual art form passed through generations. The tactile nature of clay work and artisanal value make AI replacement irrelevant.

title: Cobblers and Shoe Repairers
exposure_rationale: Shoe repair is entirely manual work requiring tactile skill and improvisation. The informal, street-level nature of this trade in India has zero AI exposure.

title: Bamboo and Cane Workers
exposure_rationale: Bamboo and cane craft is traditional artisanal work requiring hand-weaving and shaping. The organic material and custom nature of products prevent any AI automation.

title: Rickshaw Pullers
exposure_rationale: Manual rickshaw pulling is pure physical labor. While the profession is declining due to motorized transport, AI is completely irrelevant to this work.

title: Ship and Port Workers
exposure_rationale: Port automation is growing globally but Indian ports still rely heavily on manual loading, container handling, and ship maintenance. Automation adoption is gradual.

title: Railway Station Porters (Coolies)
exposure_rationale: Carrying luggage at railway stations is pure physical labor. While escalators and carts reduce demand, AI has no bearing on this manual work.

title: Jute Mill Workers
exposure_rationale: Jute processing involves manual fiber separation and weaving on traditional looms. The cottage industry nature and low mechanization make AI irrelevant.

title: Brick Kiln Workers
exposure_rationale: Brick making in India is entirely manual - molding clay, stacking, and firing kilns. The migrant labor-intensive nature has zero AI exposure.

title: Toddy Tappers
exposure_rationale: Climbing palm trees to collect sap is dangerous physical work requiring balance and agility. No technology, let alone AI, can substitute for this traditional occupation.

title: Beedi Rollers
exposure_rationale: Beedi rolling by hand is piece-rate work done in homes, primarily by women. The extremely low wages and manual nature make automation economically impossible.

title: Washermen (Dhobi)
exposure_rationale: Traditional washermen wash clothes manually at ghats and press with coal irons. While washing machines reduce demand, AI has zero applicability to this physical work.

title: Cycle Rickshaw Drivers
exposure_rationale: Pedaling rickshaws through congested streets is pure physical labor. While e-rickshaws are displacing cycle rickshaws, AI has no role in this transition.

title: Mining Workers
exposure_rationale: Coal and mineral mining in India involves manual extraction and heavy machinery operation. AI aids in geological surveys but underground manual labor persists.

title: Ayah and Hospital Attendants
exposure_rationale: Hospital ward helpers assist patients with bathing, feeding, and mobility. The intimate physical care and emotional support involved cannot be replicated by AI.

title: Panchayat Secretaries
exposure_rationale: Village-level governance involves record keeping and coordinating government schemes. Digital governance initiatives could automate data entry but community engagement remains human.

title: Astrologers and Pandits
exposure_rationale: While AI astrology apps exist, personal consultations, horoscope matching for marriages, and ritual guidance require trusted human practitioners in Indian culture.

title: Driving Instructors
exposure_rationale: Teaching driving requires physical presence, real-time corrections, and adapting to chaotic Indian road conditions. Simulators exist but cannot replace in-car instruction.

title: Survey and Census Workers
exposure_rationale: Door-to-door data collection for surveys and census requires navigating Indian localities and communicating in local languages. Digital tools help but human enumerators remain essential.

title: Goldsmith and Silver Workers
exposure_rationale: Jewelry making is an artisanal craft requiring intricate hand skills, design sensibility, and customer customization. CAD tools assist but handcraft remains valued.

title: Sign Painters and Billboard Workers
exposure_rationale: AI design tools can generate billboard layouts and signage. Digital printing has already displaced many hand-painters. However, some niche hand-painted work persists.

title: Pest Control Workers
exposure_rationale: Pest control requires physical inspection, chemical application, and site-specific judgement. AI can optimize scheduling but the hands-on work remains entirely manual.
